name: info@teamkennedy.com
subject: Our freedoms, our rights, Happy 4th of July
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TEAM KENNEDY
body:
On this day, 247 years ago, 56 brave patriots risked their lives to sign

| [ ](http://www.kennedy24.com/?e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=happy_4th_of_july&n=1)
---
Peter, On this Fourth of July, I’d like to invoke a different American theme than what we are used to. The fireworks and glorification of war is but one aspect of the American psyche. The time has come to elevate another one – the aspiration expressed in songs like “America the Beautiful” and “This Land is Your Land.” [](https://www.kennedy24.com/donate_4th?e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=happy_4th_of_july&n=2 "Freedom, RFK, Jr. and Happiness 2024") Despite decades of warlike behavior abroad and environmental destruction at home, America remains an incredibly beautiful nation. Much health and vitality still remains in our “purple mountains majesty,” our “amber waves of grain,” our broad forests, rivers, wetlands, and coasts. Underneath the warlike behavior of past decades still remains a reservoir of goodwill that seeks to bring peace to the world and to avoid the “foreign entanglements” and “adventures abroad” our Founders warned against, except when compelled by dire necessity. On this Fourth of July, let us celebrate the beauty of America and the basic goodness of the American people. Let us resolve to hold these precious, and to live up to the magnificent future that they promise. This land is your land. This land is my land. Please join me in making it the best it can possibly be..**** | [](https://www.kennedy24.com/donate_4th?amount=5&e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=happy_4th_of_july&n=3 "Give $5 ->") [Give $5 ->](https://www.kennedy24.com/donate_4th?amount=5&e=d33e868971601c1c714cfe0555abd394&utm_source=joinkennedy&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=happy_4th_of_july&n=3)

clean_body: On this day, 247 years ago, 56 brave patriots risked their lives to sign | Peter, On this Fourth of July, I’d like to invoke a different American theme than what we are used to. The fireworks and glorification of war is but one aspect of the American psyche. The time has come to elevate another one – the aspiration expressed in songs like “America the Beautiful” and “This Land is Your Land.” "Freedom, RFK, Jr. and Happiness 2024" Despite decades of warlike behavior abroad and environmental destruction at home, America remains an incredibly beautiful nation. Much health and vitality still remains in our “purple mountains majesty,” our “amber waves of grain,” our broad forests, rivers, wetlands, and coasts. Underneath the warlike behavior of past decades still remains a reservoir of goodwill that seeks to bring peace to the world and to avoid the “foreign entanglements” and “adventures abroad” our Founders warned against, except when compelled by dire necessity. On this Fourth of July, let us celebrate the beauty of America and the basic goodness of the American people. Let us resolve to hold these precious, and to live up to the magnificent future that they promise. This land is your land. This land is my land. Please join me in making it the best it can possibly be..** | "Give $5 ->" "Give $27 ->" "Give $50 ->" "Give $100 ->" "Other Amount ->" "Freedom on the 4th of July" Happy 4th of July! body:
247 years of independence.

[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/65933772/414910971/392002013?refcode=EM_C_230704Fourth_TEAM_90DO_5_NG_B_Logo&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDU1NzIiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiN2FmY2NhNzgtOGExYS1lZTExLWE5YmItMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=S5rtJy3PcZo415MDvE6GxmwsX18bhM9hbXiVPsGnjaM=)
Fellow Democrats, Happy Independence Day!

Two hundred and forty-seven years ago, Congress and the Founding Fathers declared our independence from the British Monarchy, beginning the great experiment of American Democracy that continues today.

As recent years have made all too clear, the work to protect and better that democracy is ongoing. It is up to each and every one of us to safeguard the principles that make our nation great: the principles of justice, liberty, and equality.

While we enjoy the fireworks, parades, and barbecues that mark this special day, let us hold in our hearts the shared values that unite us as Americans. Let us celebrate not just the past but also the American promise of a brighter future for all. Together, we can continue to make our great nation a beacon of hope, opportunity, and progress.

Wishing you a safe and joyful Fourth of July!

Team IDCCA

Happy Independence Day, Peter!

Every year on this day, communities across the country celebrate our nation's birthday and the freedoms afforded to us.

So as your family, friends, and neighbors gather to enjoy a barbeque and celebrate our nation's founding, let us unite and continue to honor the legacy of everyone who fought to make this day and our freedoms possible.

**From Team Schrier to you and your loved ones, we hope you have a safe and happy Independence Day!**

Thank you,

Team Schrier

subject: Happy 4th of July!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Salud Carbajal for Congress
body:
Today we celebrate the birth of our great nation and all the freedoms that make it the strong country it is.

I never forget what an honor it is to represent you in Congress and am committed to fighting for our American values of liberty, equality, and justice.

I look forward to enjoying this day with friends, family and the residents of California’s Central Coast, and hope you will join me in celebrating the spirit of America today.

Best wishes for a safe, fun and happy Independence Day!

Sincerely,
Salud Carbajal

name: Mark Walker
subject: Happy 4th of July!
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Walker4NC
body:
I’m proud to celebrate our independence and the values that make our nation great! We have so much to be grateful for in this country. | [Unsubscribe](https://click.walker4nc.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=ddf9d2682c1146b5a5fa5e9b2b9c6ab5&_s=6fa07e5c8f6a426b8c9759a23e117132&_t=o_KO9bPYSulotNNgh0dkevzITgDnSeGJCbZ7j53iMuO3cS9ttIyXToMGiA8xwe3Q-S3a85o2aMfjaiubXVKJYXet-3rqVVAVgomC8FQG-VE%3D) | [Mark as Junk](https://click.walker4nc.com/e/encryptedUnsubscribe?_r=ddf9d2682c1146b5a5fa5e9b2b9c6ab5&_s=6fa07e5c8f6a426b8c9759a23e117132&_t=o_KO9bPYSulotNNgh0dkevzITgDnSeGJCbZ7j53iMuO3cS9ttIyXToMGiA8xwe3Q-S3a85o2aMfjaiubXVKJYXet-3rqVVAVgomC8FQG-VE%3D)

---
Peter,

Happy 4th of July! I’m proud to celebrate our independence and the values that make our nation great! We have so much to be grateful for in this country.

Wishing you and your families a safe, happy, and blessed Independence Day!

| Meet **Mark Walker**

Congressman Mark Walker started his career in finance and, after earning his theology degree, Walker served 16 years as a pastor. In 2014, Walker stepped away from ministry and made a successful run for the U.S. Congress defeating a host of elected politicians. In 2015, Congressman Walker became the first member in an inaugural term to be elected Chair of the Republican Study Committee (RSC) - the largest caucus in Congress with 160 members and a position previously held by Vice President Pence and Congressman Jim Jordan.

In 2018, Walker was elected as Vice Chair of the entire conference, becoming the highest rated conservative to serve in the top four leadership positions in Congress. Walker is also the last member of Congress to secure a floor vote on a balanced budget amendment and led the House on HBCU initiatives winning the United Negro College Fund President’s list award. Congressman Walker served on multiple committees and in 2019, Walker was appointed to ranking member on the Committee on Homeland Security’s Intelligence and Counter-Terrorism’s subcommittee. Walker was co-chair of the bicameral prayer caucus for five years and also won the GOP MVP award as the starting pitcher on the Republican baseball team for the annual charity game.

Congressman Walker was redistricted out of his seat and stepped away from Congress in 2021. Walker ran for U.S. Senate in 2022 and was endorsed by Senators Tim Scott and James Lankford, Gov. Mike Huckabee, Hall of Fame Coach Tony Dungy and more than 200 pastors across North Carolina. Walker is currently the president of a political consulting business called Advancing Hope USA, and advocated for the two North Carolina Supreme Court candidates and 4 North Carolina Court of Appeals candidates through his independent expenditure organization, Win the Courts during this past general election. Congressman Walker and his wife Kelly, a Family Nurse Practitioner and flight trauma specialist, have been married for thirty years and have three children.

* * *

Wherever you are in our state (or traveling), we hope you're able to spend the day with those you love. **** As you celebrate with friends and family, I hope you think about how far we've come, and how much further we must go to create an Indiana and America that works for all, provides equal opportunity, and treats everyone fairly. As Democrats, we'll never stop fighting to better our state and country. Thanks for everything you do, and enjoy the day! July 4th Reflections Dear Maryland, On this 4th of July, I’ve been quietly reflecting on all stages of my career, thinking of those who made an impa
| | [](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5220?id=80948.822.1.f92095db03acf68d6f3f8a812d602543)
---
[](https://email.cloud2.secureclick.net/c/5220?id=80948.823.1.a75143888b7377e7577f5c7e064b2467&p=eyIlN0IlN0JtaW1pLXNpZ25hdHVyZSU3RCU3RCI6IjE3NDQyNDg0NC0xMjg2MDE5MjU1My04OTljODZjMGZkYTU4OWQ5YzQ3ODUwNDNlZjYxMWNmNzM4YmVmM2QzIiwiJTdCJTdCZW1haWxJZCU3RCU3RCI6IjgwOTQ4In0=) |
---
# July 4th Reflections
---
Dear Maryland, On this 4th of July, I’ve been quietly reflecting on all stages of my career, thinking of those who made an impact in my life. I think of the grocery store clerks who kept our state running during a pandemic and still offered friendly conversation. The nurses and doctors who showed up and never stopped showing up.
All the veterans I’ve served with, and the teachers who had a lasting influence in my life and the lives of so many others. I think of the youth who have shown up in the streets over the years, organizing tirelessly to bring necessary changes to our state.
I think of the seniors who need healthcare and housing, and the veterans who still sleep in our streets. All the people I’ve met over the years as I have listened to your stories and related them to my own. During a critical moment, we now have an opportunity to work alongside one another to build a stronger, more unified community. Together, we can unite Central and Western Maryland and make Congress work just as hard for you as Marylanders work for each other.
Our state truly has some of the most talented and compassionate people who work hard and play hard. The creative ideas I’ve already heard from so many of you have lifted me up and inspired me. Over the years, in addition to your friendship and support, you have given me priceless advice and helped me progress through each stage of my career, becoming a more effective advocate.
I thank you for the opportunity to serve you this July 4th. It is a responsibility I take seriously, and I know we can work together to solve problems, build community, and have fun while we get our hands dirty. By uniting, we have a chance to make history together, and build a movement capable of rising to meet the challenges we face. I hope you have a great time today with your friends and families, and I look forward to hearing your ideas on how we can guide Maryland forward together.
---
**In Gratitude,**
**Mia Mason**
---

clean_body: [] Friend, Happy 4th of July! Today, we remember our nation’s history and celebrate our independence. [] I hope wherever you are, today reminds you of our nation’s best principles: Liberty and Justice FOR ALL. May we always strive towards a more perfect union, and fight for equality for every American. I hope you are able to enjoy this holiday with your loved ones! Onward, Amanda K. Edwards Donate Now [] Amanda K. Edwards has the experience and innovative vision to move Houston forward. To learn more, visit our website. | | My Sisters and Brothers, This country has blessed me with countless opportunities. As the proud daughter of a factory worker and public-school teacher, I cherish what America has bestowed upon me, and I vow to continue fighting for the rights we all hold most dear. As you spend time today enjoying cookouts with family and friends, take time to appreciate how far we’ve come since the founding of our great nation, and recognize how much farther we need to go in our pursuit for liberty, justice, and true equality. Happy July 4th! And together, #WeRepresent!
---

The first African-American woman in Wisconsin history to serve in the U.S. House of Representatives, Congresswoman Gwen Moore, took her official oath of office on January 4, 2005. She served in the Wisconsin State Senate from 1993-2004. Before her election to the Senate, Congresswoman Moore served two consecutive terms in the Wisconsin State Assembly from 1989-92.

name: Cori Bush
subject: This July 4th, our country is in a period of crisis.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cori Bush for Congress
body:
Anti-freedom Republicans have made it their mission to interfere in our basic decisions about our bodies, from abortions to gender-affirming care. | |
---
Peter, The Declaration of Independence says that “all men are created equal,” referring only to wealthy white men. When the U.S. Constitution was drafted a decade later, women, Black and brown folks, and all marginalized groups were intentionally written out. This July 4th, and the upcoming 100th anniversary of the ERA being introduced, these exclusions are increasingly obvious, and our country is in crisis. Anti-freedom Republicans have made it their mission to interfere in our own decisions about our bodies, from abortions to gender-affirming care. Last year, the far-right Supreme Court overturned _Roe v. Wade_ and just last week, they ruled against Affirmative Action. **So I’m pushing for the Equal Rights Amendment to enshrine gender equality into our legal bedrock: the U.S. Constitution.** As the 28th Amendment to our Constitution, it would advance pay equity, address sexual harassment and violence, protect reproductive autonomy, and prohibit discrimination against LGBTQ+ and pregnant people -- all critical rights for every woman, and especially important for Black and brown women. [**This July 4th, please add your name to call for a country that lives up to its proclamation of freedom, by recognizing the Equal Rights Amendment as the 28th Amendment to the Constitution.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnmlf9yb9ilux69QpyOCfiDuUQ-7jJ1lww1xrWka_rnytyfe4oOsg73ZBh_OzI0HpDdQ2DmImna1cxWw8hWSRB3SQGVM0y1VkLhdjShiLRNX196H5GL5-u9ZkKkk0QhhzYhPxq-bpWPj8abkBsboxmyBpBNTFrYuPdJZPYfVTyumoWgU2XNk0VDrIxqdR4D_C0K9fA4kAWXanhsMuHof9e7_9CBqL02aaNPkW1EXCgV0hpk1qB6cr23W1jb6bh6scobGvzqOPyiAfy3Fv-RtXpe9pLgTN7M146HGGf7YXFawFqVMWCjmWZ069dbUTRH9Dew_qjBRyPigpOifEYAM8qcmWjGNG3LcLJ-SWdyvVgAKXWfR8z2b5Fdt2HeFN94J3hgVFy3PJdZlE5z7dDuGaLmQY9MyeWjdAFmXzeK2M6HM5TA8xDPF3zQhFJW7lr57T80ZNauZYW0e5DikqCQNFBDmq3pV0J-dEiomj0EtQcaFtg/3xl/_g3QBtJRROSuEOi_HfoveA/h0/1wQWw5JcLU4JDF2mcWDquft70P3AuZvb0IpLHdByF5s)**** **[ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnmlf9yb9ilux69QpyOCfiDuUQ-7jJ1lww1xrWka_rnytyfe4oOsg73ZBh_OzI0HpDdQ2DmImna1cxWw8hWSRB3SQGVM0y1VkLhdjShiLRNX196H5GL5-u9ZkKkk0QhhzYhPxq-bpWPj8abkBsboxmyBpBNTFrYuPdJZPYfVTyumoWgU2XNk0VDrIxqdR4D_C0L8ypZ21RzHk02RFCg1suKFVDGXyq989zkSVZGQH_j2eldBpzTRy_ZcdbR2iaXSK08uTyDVqWpvIbuik0mQRn5xocZ-3VYzZ6fQ10NF3_66cEgV7X-M_NlrRTjjkyM315G7MxqicPZH2VQS5uSruXFUoFlRbOTNGxBvFJYupwy7zvMiRKpyXur-MVgGcCkjIe2R4wmqdBseZzqoUEfv94criYO243sbIXhmmAvD-Cm4I5-KFYtjWmO2EIP3xo9jWzI4KSTI4CrWPjRHKLU7x530J8GyMTpxKoLxr6mDwSnSYQ/3xl/_g3QBtJRROSuEOi_HfoveA/h1/vxQCbX4dOl3Adk6cX-4vxwjkeE1xybeNsx-kHhEdjws) ** As a Black woman who has experienced domestic violence, sexual harassment in the workplace, unequal pay, and blatant gender discrimination, I know the impact constitutional equality will have on me and millions of women and queer people across this country. Once the ERA is enshrined in our Constitution, it will be an important tool for policymakers and the courts to challenge homophobic, transphobic, and anti-abortion legislation. **A constitutional amendment is more powerful than legislation. Only another constitutional amendment could revoke it, not a future President or whoever controls Congress or the Supreme Court.** And our movement has already met the requirements outlined in Article V of the Constitution to make the ERA the 28th Amendment. The ERA was passed by 2/3rds of Congress and it has now been ratified by 38 states. We only need a few more steps to make it official! **So this year, my sister-in-service Ayanna Pressley and I launched the first-ever Congressional Caucus for the Equal Rights Amendment.** We’re building the movement in Congress to recognize the 28th Amendment and to center the people who stand to benefit the most from gender equality, including Black and brown women, LGBTQ+ people, people seeking abortion care, and other marginalized groups. [**Please sign the petition this July 4th to affirm our freedom over our own bodies and support the Equal Rights Amendment as part of our Constitution.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/P8Elou2Rvc0qoMPEUZrMXcOpy_XqN4R2ZGjoK-5zZnmlf9yb9ilux69QpyOCfiDuUQ-7jJ1lww1xrWka_rnytyfe4oOsg73ZBh_OzI0HpDdQ2DmImna1cxWw8hWSRB3SQGVM0y1VkLhdjShiLRNX196H5GL5-u9ZkKkk0QhhzYhPxq-bpWPj8abkBsboxmyBpBNTFrYuPdJZPYfVTyumoWgU2XNk0VDrIxqdR4D_C0LL93dhMeUT2X-GTMwH03qVW2rz5IK1a7SVEHDqI83TyFlmV9p_xkmWdGHMoX4d5ww2qCqTUsUNVf0lljL5yFYG2lvhYo88WUGL0iQL9zCnljEZa4T5VacDXmQIrlmBFDTCvdnADdrLJ6s6sdoeHzgGYJQjh496XQM40bIoe3Q49oHDuNf3MnlTUo9fixiPiHPFEA-ft8L8fGwZUovPfeju7kAvjCTBtfqGQGklu8EnOG_e7cl10YZ5OOkq_D0TuIqdF2Ot7B1142DAbHxVIWCJDy9mX-3IstoLVa9_ey1gng/3xl/_g3QBtJRROSuEOi_HfoveA/h2/ChanR78Via45rDIq5VBYsxxyXyN0SPfYTtQ3GWT1ymc)**** Thank you. Together, we will build the future we deserve. With love, Cori **[SUPPORT OUR MOVEMENT](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/wehEm_vu1NBVXOKYSqOxTOi34aJoCJC1EivM7Ih953xYYW4y8DCxFE6qyrsvMQbj85B4omOSO0-rXQkkoTkDwV9MYxoBmARmOU8XOtWJ7ebQGALZsULUyTObOHh8z7FDIp2tS31VTmy8cHmRjF1RzHWoUuYxKkI4L6Ia2QJYDny1CJ_37kvpC5g9FJTsR5dnV-TotsgOywaVSJh3Xj3sCsQNtyd1CqOZhW5Cg-5Q6Jf5DzE2cVNxEx2mBgyXOThDoATXcXQaTY1kmy0_theqC0PVgIwTRVWjKWafzjceMZ63_qdRd0EPq7AI6js4UUi5EuGeRev_NobzE3nmQlfKVWrSNHHZbfHL8zvQ6uV1sOQo7cxkjBf_Pq9569ilNhm8czUcexrhylrWKDUX99cyas_IyPpvsb-bu0kd5bqddFVev3p5lj33hEvjkUdN-XztBHzz8_n_ip4Ma5C3j7E91Q/3xl/_g3QBtJRROSuEOi_HfoveA/h3/SyVZWNaqKFcXYnCBHSYa8fO8fB4HKD4vKeUEQJmUHyI) **

name: Yadira Caraveo
subject: Wishing you and your family a happy Independence Day!
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY CARAVEO FOR CONGRESS
body:
| [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/65935650/414917775/1561815853?refcode=em_msg_2023.07.04_b1_yc_all&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjUwOCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIwNmM2ZDg4OS05MDFhLWVlMTEtYTliYi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=55w2xYsutvLUdd135s7t7O4YlKlj3q9uqo2_KJ8gIN8=&emci=0ec73dd5-7d17-ee11-a9bb-00224832eb73&emdi=06c6d889-901a-ee11-a9bb-00224832eb73&ceid=12666102)
Hi Peter,

I wanted to reach out and wish you and your family a happy and safe Independence Day. I’ve been thinking about what this holiday means to me, and I wanted to share my thoughts with you. Today reminds me of why I first ran for office, and why I’m proud to serve Colorado’s 8th district in Congress.

**But it is also a reminder that the fight to build a more perfect union continues, Peter.** It is up to us to change what is unjust, and build on the progress we’ve made. We are the ones who will continue to shape our country into a fairer, better place, and we are the ones who move this country forward.

I sincerely hope that you’ll be able to find some time for rest, celebration, and fun over the holiday. Tomorrow, we’ll be back, more refreshed and ready to recommit ourselves to the fight for our democracy, our rights, and our future.

Thank you for standing by my side in this fight.

In solidarity,

clean_body: | Hi Peter, I wanted to reach out and wish you and your family a happy and safe Independence Day. I’ve been thinking about what this holiday means to me, and I wanted to share my thoughts with you. Today reminds me of why I first ran for office, and why I’m proud to serve Colorado’s 8th district in Congress. But it is also a reminder that the fight to build a more perfect union continues, Peter. It is up to us to change what is unjust, and build on the progress we’ve made. We are the ones who will continue to shape our country into a fairer, better place, and we are the ones who move this country forward. I sincerely hope that you’ll be able to find some time for rest, celebration, and fun over the holiday. Tomorrow, we’ll be back, more refreshed and ready to recommit ourselves to the fight for our democracy, our rights, and our future. Thank you for standing by my side in this fight. In solidarity, Yadira | PAID FOR BY CARAVEO FOR CONGRESS Caraveo for Congress PO Box 953 Eastlake, CO 80614 United States If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please .

name: Kathy Barnette
subject: Join Me for a Cocktail Reception With Presidential Candidate Vivek Ramaswamy
body:
Dear Friends, I am reaching out to invite you to a Cocktail Reception with Presidential Candidate Vivek Ramaswamy at The Residences at the Ritz Carlton Philadelphia on Friday, July 7 at 3:30 PM.Kathy Barnette is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.

Dear Friends,
I am reaching out to invite you to a Cocktail Reception with Presidential Candidate Vivek Ramaswamy at The Residences at the Ritz Carlton Philadelphia on Friday, July 7 at 3:30 PM.
Kathy Barnette is a reader-supported publication. To receive new posts and support my work, consider becoming a free or paid subscriber.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack.com/redirect/2/eyJlIjoiaHR0cHM6Ly9rYXRoeWJhcm5ldHRlLnN1YnN0YWNrLmNvbS9zdWJzY3JpYmU_.T4y_MMBYVgJRkaa22ZiP4-WmRD032aA65qMBNZ7g05E?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=131691758)
Vivek Ramaswamy is a 37-year-old graduate of Harvard, Yale Law School, a biotech entrepreneur, and a three-time author. Now he is running for President of the United States. Most recently a [Morning Consult Poll](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/c1287cb2-1248-490a-8a0e-5372925a5ce5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) with over 3,600 registered voters had Vivek polling at 6% nationwide, ahead of well-established politicians such as Tim Scott, Nikki Haley, and Chris Christie.
And just now Fox News released their latest poll that shows Vivek in 3rd place.
| [](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F105e5fb1-78a9-49de-aff8-4a56debadafa_1079x677.jpeg)|
---|---|---
Vivek's platform is centered around restoring the core values America was founded on such as meritocracy and free speech, as well as combating adversaries like the Chinese Communist Party. Some of his campaign pledges include eliminating race-based quotas, using the U.S. military to secure our southern border, shutting down the administrative bureaucracy, decoupling from the Chinese Communist Party, and most importantly restoring American civic pride in our next generation.
Vivek welcomes open dialogue and discussion, receptions such as the one next Friday are a perfect opportunity to engage with Vivek on the issues that matter most to you. Please see the attached flyer for more details as well as the link below to sign up. If you have any questions regarding this event please reach out to Dhruv Yadav at 567-698-2078 or email Dhruv at [Dyadav@vivek2024.com](mailto:Dyadav@vivek2024.com), or Kathy Barnette at [Kathy@vivek2024.com](mailto:Kathy@vivek2024.com)
[RSVP Here](https://vivekr24.com/g1mrah42)
| [](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fd1143d95-58a4-447c-a9dc-6a5e7a1c0c08_1078x1342.png)|
---|---|---
| [](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2Fdc41cead-bd62-4c45-baff-be6c299b10f9_1080x1340.png)|

clean_body: Background on report that Harvard may look for loopholes in Supreme Court ruling on affirmative action. [NEWSLINK] BREAKING NEWS... [600x300] [] Harvard University signals loophole for race-based admissions after Supreme Court says unconstitutional [] Source: Washington Examiner [] Harvard University signaled that the school might seek loopholes to the Supreme Court's decision striking down its race-based admissions scheme. Harvard President-elect Claudine Gay said the decision will change how the school conducts its admissions process but highlighted the "educational benefit of diversity" and promising "our commitment to that work remains steadfast." [13x13x1] [] READ MORE [] Background Notes from Judicial Watch [180x150x1] [] 2022-01-24 Judicial Watch Statement on Supreme Court Decision to Take Up Challenges to Harvard, UNC Race-Based Admissions Policies [] Source: Judicial Watch [] Students for Fair Admissions' petition to the Supreme Court concludes that, “UNC’s admissions process incorporated impermissible racial considerations violating the Equal Protection Clause.” [13x13x1] [] READ MORE [] [180x150x2] [] 2023-06-29 Long past time to require our higher education system to follow the Constitution [] Source: Judicial Watch [] udicial Watch President Tom Fitton: This decision could not come at a better time, as the extremist left embraces outright racial discrimination, racial separatism, and racial segregation under the guise of “anti-racism.” [13x13x1] [] READ MORE [] [180x150x3] [] 2021-04-05 Judicial Watch, Allied Educational Foundation File Brief in Case Challenging Race-Based Admissions Policies [] Source: Judicial Watch [] In their brief, Judicial Watch and AEF note that the Equal Protection Clause was passed to stop discrimination: One of the core purposes of the Equal Protection Clause is to guarantee that individuals will be free from discrimination based upon race. It should come as no surprise to anyone that legalizing the use of race in deciding who is admitted to schools of higher learning has caused enormous conflict, including among members of this Court. Each Fourth of July, we come together to celebrate the birth of the United States. But we’re not just celebrating history today, we’re taking pride in our core beliefs that distinguish our nation from the rest: Life, Liberty, and the pursuit of happiness. **Thomas Jefferson timelessly said: “For ourselves, let the annual return of this day forever refresh our recollections of these rights, and an undiminished devotion to them.” ** So whether you’re watching fireworks, going on a road trip, or having a barbecue, we hope you take a moment to reflect on our nation’s incredible history**-_and the ongoing fight we’re in together to form a more perfect union._** From the DASS family to yours: Have a happy and healthy Independence Day! **-** Travis Brimm DASS Executive Director

# **Happy Independence Day!** Let the fireworks light up the sky as we celebrate the land of the free and the home of the brave!
#
# Today, we commemorate the courage and determination of our founding fathers who fought for our liberty and set the stage for the birth of a great nation. It's a day to honor the values of freedom, equality, and justice that make America so special.

As we gather with family and friends, let's remember the sacrifices of those who came before us and reflect on the progress we've made as a nation.

Together, we have overcome challenges, united in times of adversity, and embraced the diversity that defines us. Let's use this day not just for barbecues and picnics, but also for appreciating the privilege of living in a land where dreams can become reality.

It's a reminder that the American spirit is alive and thriving, inspiring us to pursue our goals and make a difference. So, wave those flags high, wear your red, white, and blue with pride, and let freedom ring!

May this 4th of July ignite a spark within each of us to work towards a brighter future, where every American can truly experience "life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness."

[Please share this message of unity, gratitude, and patriotism with your loved ones, and let's make this Independence Day celebration one to remember!](https://gmail.us10.list-manage.com/track/click?u=6f3fd147b34df4325e5fd7c3c&id=e3d2ee4eb2&e=6d65681b04)

name: =?utf-8?Q?Tracey=20Mann?=
subject: Independence Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mann for Congress, Inc
body:
It was an honor to be in Hutchinson today to celebrate Independence Day | | |
---
| |
---
| Dear Kansan,

It was an honor to be in Hutchinson today to celebrate Independence Day and the enduring vision of the United States. In America, we have the right to speak, think, and worship as we see fit. That’s not the case for millions of people living in other parts of the world today. Independence Day is also a chance to come together around what unites us, and recognize the rich heritage we all share as citizens of the United States of America.

The values that make America great are still alive and well in Kansas, the pilot light of America. Where I come from, people live their lives by faith, family, freedom, hard work, love of neighbor, and personal responsibility. These are the values that will save America because they’re the values around which our country is based.

Together, as Americans, we can and will conquer any challenge that comes our way.

**_As you enjoy Independence Day, may God bless you and your families, and may God continue to bless the United States of America._**

In the Fight,
Tracey Mann

P.S. I stand for Kansas, and I always will. I promise that I will do everything within my power to ensure that our kids have a bright future.

---

[](https://substack.com/profile/15484941-shahid-buttar)| | Shahid Buttar| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=132829795&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTMyODI5Nzk1LCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTY4ODQ5ODc2OCwiZXhwIjoxNjkxMDkwNzY4LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.PpjsxDFke5DeHwQrUOa94pDjrJVi0FC4imn9aDOzbUA&utm_medium=email&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=132829795&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTMyODI5Nzk1LCJpYXQiOjE2ODg0OTg3NjgsImV4cCI6MTY5MTA5MDc2OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.-imgM4KYwJze89ntZLUmUUKfQk225gdfTxLkjfCKius&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=132829795&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTMyODI5Nzk1LCJpYXQiOjE2ODg0OTg3NjgsImV4cCI6MTY5MTA5MDc2OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.-imgM4KYwJze89ntZLUmUUKfQk225gdfTxLkjfCKius)
The day after Independence Day in 1852, Frederick Douglass offered a timeless speech in Rochester, New York to the The Ladies Anti-Slavery Society. It represented a resounding refutation of national myths that persist to this day.
Chief among them is [the myth that slavery ended in the United States](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/9efeb834-89d1-45bb-9d3b-69e34b76c431?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) after the Civil War, when in fact the 13th Amendment to the U.S. Constitution explicitly invites it "as a condition of punishment." Today, in 2023, more Americans remain legally enslaved than were held in bondage at the height of the Antebellum South.
My newsletter aims to educate, inform, and inspire. This post is free, so share it with anyone who could learn form it!
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=132829795&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTMyODI5Nzk1LCJpYXQiOjE2ODg0OTg3NjgsImV4cCI6MTY5MTA5MDc2OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.-imgM4KYwJze89ntZLUmUUKfQk225gdfTxLkjfCKius)
As our nation celebrates Independence Day, it would behoove each of us to consider the reality that too many Americans either never learn, or instead remain too afraid to acknowledge.
"There is not a nation on the Earth guilty of practices, more shocking and bloody, than are the people of these United States, at this very hour."
-- Frederick Douglass
### What to the Slave Is the Fourth of July?
Douglass' speech was titled "[What to the Slave Is the Fourth of July?](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/06a09417-041e-4478-ae2f-e99ae49dabea?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)" While he delivered his oration roughly a decade before the Civil War, his analysis remains painfully appropriate today, as millions of Americans celebrate the "independence" of a country built on [genocide](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/c38f838e-cf53-4272-be26-cdc85ec52906?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and forced labor that is the only one in human history to ever deploy nuclear weapons--against civilian populations, at that.
He began by observing the bravery of the revolutionaries who rejected colonial rule, and the timeless vision expressed by our nation's founding documents.
> Feeling themselves harshly and unjustly treated by the home government, your fathers, like men of honesty, and men of spirit, earnestly sought redress. They petitioned and remonstrated; they did so in a decorous, respectful, and loyal manner. Their conduct was wholly unexceptionable. This, however, did not answer the purpose. They saw themselves treated with sovereign indifference, coldness and scorn. Yet they persevered. They were not the men to look back....
> Oppression makes a wise man mad. Your fathers were wise men, and if they did not go mad, they became restive under this treatment. They felt themselves the victims of grievous wrongs, wholly incurable in their colonial capacity. With brave men there is always a remedy for oppression....It was a startling idea...The timid and the prudent...of that day, were, of course, shocked and alarmed by it....
> Pride and patriotism, not less than gratitude, prompt you to celebrate and to hold it in perpetual remembrance. I have said that [the Declaration of Independence](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/2c2a835f-1301-4c76-b631-0dd28df53336?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is the ring-bolt to the chain of your nation's destiny; so, indeed, I regard it. The principles contained in that instrument are saving principles. Stand by those principles, be true to them on all occasions, in all places, against all foes, and at whatever cost....
It bears noting that the [Vietnamese Declaration of Independence](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/2afb867c-5461-4855-bd05-56b68ab672e6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) authored by Ho Chi Minh in 1945 began by quoting its American precursor that helped inspire it. In response, America invaded, killing millions and dropping more bombs on the neighboring countries of [Laos](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/6225e5ee-2145-48d3-b29e-fc36b78cb382?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and [Cambodia](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/a563e503-3e62-441a-b2ca-e47aa5dfb0cb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (with whom Washington claimed not to be at war) than were deployed during the entire period of the Second World War. Unexploded ordnance continued to kill and main for decades, and the ecocidal deployment of defoliant Agent Orange led to birth deformities and preventable illnesses not only among Vietnamese several generations later, but also the children of U.S. servicemembers treated like cannon fodder to serve the whims of Wall Street.
That was far from the last time that our country would betray our founding principles.
If you found this post or podcast informative, sign up for a (free or paid) subscription to keep learning.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..PJqmF8v9MwIRX3O-WKrXkp50RY1-6EwePRGBVw2xQKQ?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=132829795)
Douglass then turned from observing the bravery of revolutionaries and the value of our nation's founding documents to the unfortunate reality of his era--and our's today. He continued:
> I am not included within the pale of this glorious anniversary! Your high independence only reveals the immeasurable distance between us. The blessings in which you, this day, rejoice, are not enjoyed in common. The rich inheritance of justice, liberty, prosperity and independence, bequeathed by your fathers, is shared by you, not by me. The sunlight that brought life and healing to you, has brought stripes and death to me. This Fourth [of] July is yours, not mine.
> You may rejoice, I must mourn. To drag a man in fetters into the grand illuminated temple of liberty, and call upon him to join you in joyous anthems, were inhuman mockery and sacrilegious irony....
> My subject, then fellow-citizens, is American Slavery. I shall see, this day, and its popular characteristics, from the slave's point of view. Standing, there, identified with the American bondman, making his wrongs mine, I do not hesitate to declare, with all my soul, that the character and conduct of this nation never looked blacker to me than on this 4th of July!...
> What, am I to argue that it is wrong to make men brutes, to rob them of their liberty, to work them without wages, to keep them ignorant of their relations to their fellow men, to beat them with sticks, to flay their flesh...to sunder their families, to knock out their teeth, to burn their flesh, to starve them into obedience and submission to their masters?
[A brief digression here I would simply add: "or, perhaps, to kneel on their necks"?]
> Must I argue that a system thus marked with blood, and stained with pollution, is wrong?...
> At a time like this, scorching irony, not convincing argument, is needed. O! had I the ability, and could I reach the nation's ear, I would, to-day, pour out a fiery stream of biting ridicule, blasting reproach, withering sarcasm, and stern rebuke. For it is not light that is needed, but fire; it is not the gentle shower, but thunder. We need the storm, the whirlwind, and the earthquake. The feeling of the nation must be quickened; the conscience of the nation must be roused; the propriety of the nation must be startled; the hypocrisy of the nation must be exposed; and its crimes against God and man must be proclaimed and denounced.
> What, to the American slave, is your 4th of July? I answer: a day that reveals to him, more than all other days in the year, the gross injustice and cruelty to which he is the constant victim. To him, your celebration is a sham; your boasted liberty, an unholy license; your national greatness, swelling vanity; your sounds of rejoicing are empty and heartless; your denunciations of tyrants, brass fronted impudence; your shouts of liberty and equality, hollow mockery; your prayers and hymns, your sermons and thanksgivings, with all your religious parade, and solemnity, are, to him, mere bombast, fraud, deception, impiety, and hypocrisy - a thin veil to cover up crimes which would disgrace a nation of savages.
> There is not a nation on the Earth guilty of practices, more shocking and bloody, than are the people of these United States, at this very hour. Go where you may, search where you will, roam through all the monarchies and despotisms of the old world, travel through South America, search out every abuse, and when you have found the last, lay your facts by the side of the [everyday practices of this nation](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/5b1adaf8-e5ff-4e03-9b58-c1ac633fc054?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and you will say with me, that, for revolting barbarity and shameless hypocrisy, America reigns without a rival.
It is difficult to muster respect for a country wielding exorbitant resources, an implacable dominance of weapons, and a self-marginalizing ignorance of its own stated principles. Across the world--from 1852 and still to this day--no country embodies that combination better than does the United States.
### Bipartisan imperialism continues under Biden
Some might imagine that Douglass' observations were historically accurate, but have been superseded by intervening events. They would unfortunately be wrong.
In the years since Frederick Douglass observed the casual brutality with which our country once enslaved millions of Black Americans, the United States has:
* embraced an [industrial labor strategy](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/3367d2d1-1ffa-4659-b12c-e1f53e2fefad?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) based on slave labor in prisons;
* reversed its most significant legal protections for civil rights and civil liberties from [voting rights](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/62ad98fe-d6cd-47ce-9f8a-18f51655fece?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to [reproductive rights](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/104f98a7-7f2b-46d9-8cd0-65931ec75470?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg);
* [contrived](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/e9983cdc-f565-428b-8b1d-c8e543cf806f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) an unprecedented upward [transfer](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/ab36fbaa-c5db-4a66-b1de-ab34a029704e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of [wealth](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/cfccfa7c-4cbc-475a-82c0-49a1d187788d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) from the [poor and middle class to the rich](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/43aafac7-33cf-47ee-ba15-6bcf83976ed2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg);
* invaded (or sponsored violent coups d'etat) in [dozens of countries](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/e54ecc8c-8d0c-41a2-858a-7d3a77cac0f3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) around the world, including several that have tried to nationalize their natural (eg [fossil fuel](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/5e8e054e-01fe-4833-b7ac-7d821bfce96a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [fruit](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/d2502ff9-3d42-4c3f-aabc-69f9e640fb2f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), [lithium](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/b1915b88-f4a5-4857-9c9a-35dbc09c707d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and other) resources to reclaim them from multinational companies;
* eroded the international legal regime we once fought a world war to establish by [promoting torturers](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/af9f76dc-fb4b-4286-a6cc-5be25b0420ce?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) rather than [holding them accountable](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/06d93ebc-402f-411d-a9f8-199f977f6b0e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and placing international criminals to public advisory bodies despite [decades of human rights abuses](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/4eb2aa7e-9849-48fe-a8a7-dd67a79e8469?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) (which Biden did just yesterday); and
* [engineered](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/23edde0f-c3e2-45ac-8370-a1beaef50c8f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) a global climate catastrophe to fill the pockets of fossil fuel companies (which Congress [continues](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/a4b6a35a-32ab-4288-a7ef-7e70c55fd518?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to idiotically subsidize using public funds).

Every one of those decisions was an object of thoroughly bipartisan support.
### Power concedes nothing without a demand
5 years after his historic speech addressing the Fourth of July and reality of American despotism, Frederick Douglass again [made history](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/8a696288-0c19-4a4f-9805-c63826b9ec71?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by observing the pattern of deference that enables it. in some respects, his prescience anticipated the much later wisdom of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr., whose canonical Letter from Birmingham Jail paraphrased much of Douglass' analysis.
Speaking on August 3, 1857 in Canandaigua, New York, he said:
> Let me give you a word of the philosophy of reform. The whole history of the progress of human liberty shows that all concessions yet made to her august claims have been born of earnest struggle. The conflict has been exciting, agitating, all-absorbing, and for the time being, putting all other tumults to silence. It must do this or it does nothing. If there is no struggle there is no progress. Those who profess to favor freedom and yet deprecate agitation are men who want crops without plowing up the ground; they want rain without thunder and lightning. They want the ocean without the awful roar of its many waters.
> This struggle may be a moral one, or it may be a physical one, and it may be both moral and physical, but it must be a struggle. Power concedes nothing without a demand. It never did and it never will. Find out just what any people will quietly submit to and you have found out the exact measure of injustice and wrong which will be imposed upon them, and these will continue till they are resisted with either words or blows, or with both. The limits of tyrants are prescribed by the endurance of those whom they oppress.
Having myself made public demands of an unjust system and discovered its continuing and bipartisan [racist corruption](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/bc81534d-e72a-42e1-bd86-b914f4d0161c?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the hard way, I recognize the blatant inadequacy of whatever passes for the so-called "left" in the United States. From public interest organizations that settle for advocating before ultimately co-opted courts to constituent groups pleading for reforms from career and dynasty politicians who ignore and co-opt them, even most voices critical of American despotism sadly continue to enable it.
In that context, actual demands--of the sort that force power to consider concessions--are few and far between. That's one reason to embrace them on the rare occasion that they do appear, and to recognize the profundity of those opportunities when they emerge.
Beyond my work in the courts and non-profit policy advocacy, I'm most proud of my projects that created those kinds of opportunities, from organizing direct action to shut down the facilities of weapons manufacturers to a campaign that offered San Francisco its first contested congressional election in 35 years. I'm even more proud of an intellectually magnificent mentor, muse, and voice of moral conscience who stands in the proud steps of Frederick Douglass today: Dr. Cornel West.
Dr. West's candidacy for the White House represents far more than a presidential campaign. It embodies precisely the kind of demand that Frederick Douglass invited. [Dr. West exposes the fraud on which corporate Democrats and President Joe Biden have long relied](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/c76abe77-7c22-4928-bc8e-018155bd67a2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg), and is uniquely poised to force the public reckoning that they have done everything possible to pre-empt.
In his speech observing the vacuous meaning of July Fourth, Frederick Douglass began by praising democracy and the revolution that aimed to secure it. Nearly 170 years later, America has an unprecedented chance to act on the knowledge and insight that he shared.
Or instead, the Sheeple of the United States could continue to sit on their hands and bleat as democracy continues to recede around the world, the climate catastrophe continues to race their grandchildren off a cliff, and weapons manufacturers continue to laugh all the way to the bank.
In another era, a successor to Frederick Douglas and precursor to Dr. West, Malcolm X, observed that [the choice before America](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/976d1d41-e177-49f7-b210-a1bf4532c91e?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is ultimately one between "the ballot" and "the bullet." Having embraced entirely too many bullets--from a seemingly neverending series of mass shootings to an equally neverending string of wars and pretexts to sell weapons--America might dedicate this Independence Day to a demand at the ballot box that could spare us worse yet to come.
### Taking action inspired by Douglass, X, and West
Paid subscribers can watch a speech I gave in San Francisco on July 4, 2020 during the height of the protests challenging the contemporary slavery regime embedded in mass incarceration. It reflects on some of the same themes as Frederick Douglass, whose voice and legacy--from his speech 168 years ago to his public observation that "[power concedes nothing without a demand](https://link.sbstck.com/redirect/8a696288-0c19-4a4f-9805-c63826b9ec71?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)"--have crucially informed my perspective, as well as my actions.
Did this post or podcast inform you? Sign up for a (free or paid) subscription to keep learning.
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..PJqmF8v9MwIRX3O-WKrXkp50RY1-6EwePRGBVw2xQKQ?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=132829795)
Beyond standing with a liberatory social movement seeking human rights that have only further degraded in the time since I spoke in 2020, I remain especially proud to have connected the dots between domestic and international examples of American authoritarianism. To the extent my efforts as a public advocate have offered anything unique to my community and country, it is the recognition of global intersections--and opportunities for solidarity--that sadly elude most observers. ...

This Fourth of July, remember the reality that feel-good propaganda continues to obscure and hide in plain sight | | | ---|---|--- | | Shahid Buttar| | | | | | The day after Independence Day in 1852, Frederick Douglass offered a timeless speech in Rochester, New York to the The Ladies Anti-Slavery Society. It represented a resounding refutation of national myths that persist to this day. Chief among them is after the Civil War, when in fact the 13th Amendment to the U.S. Constitution explicitly invites it "as a condition of punishment." Today, in 2023, more Americans remain legally enslaved than were held in bondage at the height of the Antebellum South. My newsletter aims to educate, inform, and inspire. This post is free, so share it with anyone who could learn form it! As our nation celebrates Independence Day, it would behoove each of us to consider the reality that too many Americans either never learn, or instead remain too afraid to acknowledge. "There is not a nation on the Earth guilty of practices, more shocking and bloody, than are the people of these United States, at this very hour." -- Frederick Douglass What to the Slave Is the Fourth of July? Douglass' speech was titled "" While he delivered his oration roughly a decade before the Civil War, his analysis remains painfully appropriate today, as millions of Americans celebrate the "independence" of a country built on and forced labor that is the only one in human history to ever deploy nuclear weapons--against civilian populations, at that. He began by observing the bravery of the revolutionaries who rejected colonial rule, and the timeless vision expressed by our nation's founding documents. Feeling themselves harshly and unjustly treated by the home government, your fathers, like men of honesty, and men of spirit, earnestly sought redress. They petitioned and remonstrated; they did so in a decorous, respectful, and loyal manner. Their conduct was wholly unexceptionable. This, however, did not answer the purpose. They saw themselves treated with sovereign indifference, coldness and scorn. Yet they persevered. They were not the men to look back.... Oppression makes a wise man mad. Your fathers were wise men, and if they did not go mad, they became restive under this treatment. They felt themselves the victims of grievous wrongs, wholly incurable in their colonial capacity. With brave men there is always a remedy for oppression....It was a startling idea...The timid and the prudent...of that day, were, of course, shocked and alarmed by it.... Pride and patriotism, not less than gratitude, prompt you to celebrate and to hold it in perpetual remembrance. I have said that is the ring-bolt to the chain of your nation's destiny; so, indeed, I regard it. The principles contained in that instrument are saving principles. Stand by those principles, be true to them on all occasions, in all places, against all foes, and at whatever cost.... It bears noting that the authored by Ho Chi Minh in 1945 began by quoting its American precursor that helped inspire it. In response, America invaded, killing millions and dropping more bombs on the neighboring countries of and (with whom Washington claimed not to be at war) than were deployed during the entire period of the Second World War. Unexploded ordnance continued to kill and main for decades, and the ecocidal deployment of defoliant Agent Orange led to birth deformities and preventable illnesses not only among Vietnamese several generations later, but also the children of U.S. servicemembers treated like cannon fodder to serve the whims of Wall Street. That was far from the last time that our country would betray our founding principles. If you found this post or podcast informative, sign up for a (free or paid) subscription to keep learning. Douglass then turned from observing the bravery of revolutionaries and the value of our nation's founding documents to the unfortunate reality of his era--and our's today. He continued: I am not included within the pale of this glorious anniversary! Your high independence only reveals the immeasurable distance between us. The blessings in which you, this day, rejoice, are not enjoyed in common. The rich inheritance of justice, liberty, prosperity and independence, bequeathed by your fathers, is shared by you, not by me. The sunlight that brought life and healing to you, has brought stripes and death to me. This Fourth [of] July is yours, not mine. You may rejoice, I must mourn. To drag a man in fetters into the grand illuminated temple of liberty, and call upon him to join you in joyous anthems, were inhuman mockery and sacrilegious irony.... My subject, then fellow-citizens, is American Slavery. I shall see, this day, and its popular characteristics, from the slave's point of view. Standing, there, identified with the American bondman, making his wrongs mine, I do not hesitate to declare, with all my soul, that the character and conduct of this nation never looked blacker to me than on this 4th of July!... What, am I to argue that it is wrong to make men brutes, to rob them of their liberty, to work them without wages, to keep them ignorant of their relations to their fellow men, to beat them with sticks, to flay their flesh...to sunder their families, to knock out their teeth, to burn their flesh, to starve them into obedience and submission to their masters? [A brief digression here I would simply add: "or, perhaps, to kneel on their necks"?] Must I argue that a system thus marked with blood, and stained with pollution, is wrong?... At a time like this, scorching irony, not convincing argument, is needed. O! had I the ability, and could I reach the nation's ear, I would, to-day, pour out a fiery stream of biting ridicule, blasting reproach, withering sarcasm, and stern rebuke. For it is not light that is needed, but fire; it is not the gentle shower, but thunder. We need the storm, the whirlwind, and the earthquake. The feeling of the nation must be quickened; the conscience of the nation must be roused; the propriety of the nation must be startled; the hypocrisy of the nation must be exposed; and its crimes against God and man must be proclaimed and denounced. What, to the American slave, is your 4th of July? I answer: a day that reveals to him, more than all other days in the year, the gross injustice and cruelty to which he is the constant victim. To him, your celebration is a sham; your boasted liberty, an unholy license; your national greatness, swelling vanity; your sounds of rejoicing are empty and heartless; your denunciations of tyrants, brass fronted impudence; your shouts of liberty and equality, hollow mockery; your prayers and hymns, your sermons and thanksgivings, with all your religious parade, and solemnity, are, to him, mere bombast, fraud, deception, impiety, and hypocrisy - a thin veil to cover up crimes which would disgrace a nation of savages. There is not a nation on the Earth guilty of practices, more shocking and bloody, than are the people of these United States, at this very hour. Go where you may, search where you will, roam through all the monarchies and despotisms of the old world, travel through South America, search out every abuse, and when you have found the last, lay your facts by the side of the , and you will say with me, that, for revolting barbarity and shameless hypocrisy, America reigns without a rival. It is difficult to muster respect for a country wielding exorbitant resources, an implacable dominance of weapons, and a self-marginalizing ignorance of its own stated principles. Across the world--from 1852 and still to this day--no country embodies that combination better than does the United States. Bipartisan imperialism continues under Biden Some might imagine that Douglass' observations were historically accurate, but have been superseded by intervening events. They would unfortunately be wrong. In the years since Frederick Douglass observed the casual brutality with which our country once enslaved millions of Black Americans, the United States has: embraced an based on slave labor in prisons; reversed its most significant legal protections for civil rights and civil liberties from to ; an unprecedented upward of from the ; invaded (or sponsored violent coups d'etat) in around the world, including several that have tried to nationalize their natural (eg , , , and other) resources to reclaim them from multinational companies; eroded the international legal regime we once fought a world war to establish by rather than , and placing international criminals to public advisory bodies despite (which Biden did just yesterday); and a global climate catastrophe to fill the pockets of fossil fuel companies (which Congress to idiotically subsidize using public funds). body:
I will always fight to uphold the ideals of freedom for all of us | | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/65951586/414929724/2121138121?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDA1NzQiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiMjBkNGZhYmQtYTAxYS1lZTExLWE5YmItMDAyMjQ4MzJlYjczIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=j_g0keqvP4OMLrMFxdI1MWAA-c0X1LlNA0wGvjVcmfk=&emci=872b57b7-ae16-ee11-a9bb-00224832eb73&emdi=20d4fabd-a01a-ee11-a9bb-00224832eb73&ceid=11003312)
---
Peter,

Happy July 4th!

Today, we celebrate America’s independence and reflect on the work our nation has accomplished and the work that lies ahead.

I will always fight to uphold the ideals of freedom for all of us, and now more than ever, it’s important we renew our commitment to our democracy. We must continue fighting for our values and uphold our promise to create a nation that promises freedom for us all – freedom to vote without hindrance, freedom to make our own health care choices, and freedom to express ourselves and our identities however we want.

I hope you have a safe and enjoyable holiday, and as you celebrate with your friends and family today, please keep our hardworking firefighters in mind. Do your part to limit sparks on the landscape. Firefighters have our backs, so let’s have theirs!

Happy Independence Day,

Hilary
---

name: Senator Katie Britt
subject: Happy Independence Day, Peter!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Britt for Alabama, Inc
body:
I’m grateful to be American–today and every day.

Peter,

When Thomas Jefferson put pen to paper 247 years ago today and wrote the
now-immortal words of the Declaration of Independence, an experiment began
unlike any other the world had seen.

A nation was created on the principle of freedom–that all are created equal,
endowed by their Creator with certain unalienable rights.

Between then and now, our nation has sent a man to the moon, won two World
Wars, annihilated evil across the globe, and become the top producer of
opportunity in the world.

We’ve come a long way since 1776, but one thing remains the same: the United
States is still the greatest country on Earth.

I know that our nation’s best days are still ahead of us, and I’m fighting
every day to ensure that the experiment started in 1776 continues to be a
success.

Happy Independence Day, Peter. I’m so grateful for your help in fighting for
the future of our beloved country.

In Freedom,

Senator Katie Britt

Paid for by Britt for Alabama, Inc.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com <mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com>.

name: Bob Casey for Senate
subject: Happy Independence Day
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BOB CASEY FOR SENATE INC
body:
# [](https://go.bobcasey.com/go/181?t=1&akid=251%2E552475%2ER-hHz2)
Peter, Happy Independence Day.
247 years ago, the Second Continental Congress gathered at Independence Hall on Chestnut Street in Philadelphia and approved the Declaration of Independence. With its adoption, the body solidified a commitment to fundamental democratic ideals that would make America a beacon of hope in the world.
**America is not just a country — it's a promise.** Generation after generation, we have fought to fulfill that promise, from the great victories of the civil rights movement to our fellow Americans who put their lives on the line and died to defend our sacred rights and freedoms.
But the fight is not over. The Nation we celebrate on July 4th is one where we believe that every person is created equal and has inalienable rights to life, liberty, and the pursuit of happiness – and we still have work to do to make our country live up to the ideals enshrined in that great Declaration.
Today, we hope your celebration is filled with family, fun, and fireworks.
Bob Casey for Senate

name: =?utf-8?Q?Charlotte=20Bergmann.?=
subject: A Reason to Celebrate INDEPENDENCE DAY!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Bergmann for Congress Campaign
body:
Please take the Republican Presidential Poll! | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/9eaa9a65b5d1/independenceday2023-5403610?e=9dd54a4530)
---
|
---
Peter, **From 1776 to the present day, July 4th has been celebrated as the birth of American independence, with festivities ranging from fireworks, parades and concerts to more casual family gatherings and barbecues.** It's easy, however, to take liberty for granted, and to misconstrue just how difficult it was to gain our freedoms 247 years ago. The Revolutionary War was long and costly--arguably the second-longest conflict in American history. While the 50,000 or so casualties on the American side are roughly equal in number to the total dead in WWI, this was at a time when there were fewer than three million people were living in the former British colonies. So, by all means, march in a parade, host a barbecue, have a few beers, head to the beach, light off some fireworks. My family and I will be right there with you in spirit. But take a minute or two to reflect on why we celebrate as well. As Thomas Paine quoted: "_Those who expect to reap the blessings of freedom, must, like men, undergo the fatigue of supporting it_." Although things may look bleak, and the MSM news often points out mostly negative things which makes you sad, know this, **God has not given up on America, and we will come out of this period, celebrating in the land of the free!**
Sincerely,
Charlotte Bergmann
| |

Peter,
I wanted to take a moment to reach out to each and every one of you to celebrate this significant day in our nation's history. Independence Day is a time for us to reflect on the principles that have shaped our great nation and the remarkable progress we have made as a people.

First and foremost, I would like to extend my heartfelt gratitude to all the servicemen and women, past and present, who have dedicated their lives to protecting the freedoms we hold dear. Their unwavering commitment to our nation's security deserves our utmost respect and admiration.

Independence Day is a time to come together as a community and celebrate the values that unite us. It is a day to remember the bravery and sacrifices of our founding fathers, who had the vision and courage to declare independence from tyranny, and to establish a government that is accountable to its citizens.

Wishing you and your loved ones a safe and joyous Fourth of July!

Sincerely,

clean_body: | | | | 🇺🇸 Peter, I wanted to take a moment to reach out to each and every one of you to celebrate this significant day in our nation's history. Independence Day is a time for us to reflect on the principles that have shaped our great nation and the remarkable progress we have made as a people. First and foremost, I would like to extend my heartfelt gratitude to all the servicemen and women, past and present, who have dedicated their lives to protecting the freedoms we hold dear. Their unwavering commitment to our nation's security deserves our utmost respect and admiration. Independence Day is a time to come together as a community and celebrate the values that unite us. It is a day to remember the bravery and sacrifices of our founding fathers, who had the vision and courage to declare independence from tyranny, and to establish a government that is accountable to its citizens. Wishing you and your loved ones a safe and joyous Fourth of July! Sincerely, Lori Chavez-DeRemer Congresswoman OR-05 | | | | | | | | Even as a Congresswoman, Lori’s most important title is mom. That’s how she knows the issues your family faces, and that’s why she fights everyday to make life better for every Oregon family, and every American. Aside from that, all you need to know are the issues she’s fighting for in Congress: If that sounds like the kind of agenda you can get behind, If you’d prefer to give by check, please use the below address: Lori Chavez-DeRemer for Congress 13203 SE 172nd Ave Suite 166 #399 Happy Valley, OR 97086 Forward This message to FIVE FRIENDS AND FAMILY to spread the word on how to achieve a better future for Oregon and America → ** Also text “LORI” to 71800 or click here to receive all of Lori’s messages | | | | Lori Chavez-DeRemer 13203 SE 172nd Ave, Suite 166 #399, Happy Valley, OR 97086 = \- ==

| | Friend,

---
Today, we celebrate the adversity our great country has overcome, and we honor the blood, sweat, and tears that have been sacrificed in the name of keeping our Republic FREE.

---
But we also must recognize that freedom in America is hanging on by a mere thread under Crooked Joe Biden and the radical Left’s Third World tin-pot dictatorship.

---
_Make no mistake, 2024 is the FINAL battle._

---
Either _we_ win and restore America to its former glory (_AND MORE_), or _they_ do, and we lose our country for good.

---
Friend, there were no doubt times when our Founding Fathers faced seemingly insurmountable adversity – **_but it wasn’t in their blood to surrender_**.

---
_And now, as we face tyrannical forces who seek to destroy our country from within, our Party needs a candidate – and our country a leader – who will never bend the knee.
_
---
So, on this Independence Day, I ask that freedom-loving patriots like YOU make a pledge...

---
**...A pledge to stand with the** _**only**_**candidate who will NEVER,****_EVER_****SURRENDER our country to the Left’s tyranny.**

---
And in return, I promise _YOU_ this: **When I’m back in the White House, YOUR life and our country**** _will_****be GREAT AGAIN! **

name: Ingrid Centurion
subject: Our Nation's Benefactor Ben
body:
Hi D. P., On this day of celebration for our country's 247th birthday let's remember the amazing contributions Ben Franklin
| | **Can't see this message?_[View in a browser](https://www.generationsforamerica.com/so/bdOaYOGRF/c?w=nhzONMIhsVfGzg-aPANZQ4Ohg2qHMPTOKuB5TvykYW8.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZ2VuZXJhdGlvbnNmb3JhbWVyaWNhLmNvbS9zby9iZE9hWU9HUkY_bGFuZ3VhZ2VUYWc9ZW4iLCJtIjoibWFpbCIsImMiOiIwMzcyYjMxOS1jM2FmLTRlMTUtYTIxNi00OTAxY2E1YWQ1M2QifQ)_**

| | |

| |
---
| |
---
| |
## "Without continual growth and progress, such words as improvement, achievement, and success have no meaning." Ben Franklin
---
| |
---
|
---
| | Hi D. P., On this day of celebration for our country's 247th birthday let's remember the amazing contributions [Ben Franklin](https://www.generationsforamerica.com/so/bdOaYOGRF/c?w=7F3cR3VdDVvfylRlpqPyrcXgWS2u-SB7-lG72Yifzdk.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cudGhvdWdodGNvLmNvbS9zdG9yeS1vZi1iZW5qYW1pbi1mcmFua2xpbi0xOTg5ODUyIiwiciI6IjYyZjFiMDRhLTQ1ZGYtNDMwZC03YTRmLTgxOWQwYzQ5YzE4MCIsIm0iOiJtYWlsIiwiYyI6IjAzNzJiMzE5LWMzYWYtNGUxNS1hMjE2LTQ5MDFjYTVhZDUzZCJ9) made to the fledgling United States. The Founding Father helped draft the Declaration of Independence and the U.S. Constitution. His diplomacy brought the French into the American Revolution. He was a self-educated leader who loved reading and inventor of beneficial gadgets. He helped establish the first subscription library in the Colonies, called the Library Company of Philadelphia. His invention of bifocals engaged our attention in the profound writings of the 18th century like Thomas Paine's "Common Sense" and the U.S. Constitution's Bill of Rights. Innovators including Franklin laid the foundation for our nation's pursuit of opportunity to become an ideal of freedom for the world to emulate. His lifelong commitment to education helped unlock our nation's intellectual potential. Franklin's role model continues to inspire curious minds to excel in the sciences helping to make our communities prosper.
---
| |
---
| | Read our news at blog [https://www.generationsforamerica.com/blog](https://www.generationsforamerica.com/so/bdOaYOGRF/c?w=mJpu7Gn5HCTIH7DShhi9xnp3fuaiDRxy4L3XDNse1Ls.). Our upcoming events and programs:
* English First every Thursday night 7PM, Immaculate Conception Catholic Church, 510 St James Ave, Goose Creek, SC. Spanish class on Friday nights at 7PM.
* Parliamentarian Procedure/Robert's Rule of Order Training -Aug 1, 8, 15, 22, 29, Sep 5th (Tuesday) 7:30-8:30 ESTSign-up today for only $40 per course at [https://www.generationsforamericafoundation.com//_paylink/AYi1gILi](https://www.generationsforamerica.com/so/bdOaYOGRF/c?w=8nCOWA3p4aBAd8M9NWhgWTv6Ni1v9s39Waq8Qg9OxQc.eyJ1IjoiaHR0cHM6Ly93d3cuZ2VuZXJhdGlvbnNmb3JhbWVyaWNhZm91bmRhdGlvbi5jb20vL19wYXlsaW5rL0FZaTFnSUxpIiwiciI6ImZmYWJhOWY4LTQ0MTktNGRlMy1kMjQxLWNlYzA4MWYyN2JmYiIsIm0iOiJtYWlsIiwiYyI6IjAzNzJiMzE5LWMzYWYtNGUxNS1hMjE2LTQ5MDFjYTVhZDUzZCJ9)
* Stop the Violence Car Show and Rally, 15 Jul 2023, Pepperell Square Shopping Center, 3316 Ashley Phosphate Rd, North Charleston, start at 10AM, essay contest winner gets that cool trophy in my photo. Kids electric racing, food trucks, music and more for the whole family to enjoy. If you would like to have a table contact us.

Happy 4th of July! Ingrid Centurion

name: Team BAFAF
subject: What does freedom mean to you?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BUILD ALASKA'S FUTURE ACTION FUND
body:
Let us know what you’re celebrating on the 4th of July!

| | | [ ](https://click.everyaction.com/k/65952321/414935546/1859846009?amount=25&utm_campaign=em_230704_freedom&utm_source=em&utm_medium=em&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzEwMjc1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNTcxMjA1ZC1hYTFhLWVlMTEtYTliYi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sLKTbFx8oBYgCr05QiPh24axTdlFyjqKiz9ZcHeJ8FY=&emci=a4065aec-a61a-ee11-a9bb-00224832eb73&emdi=1571205d-aa1a-ee11-a9bb-00224832eb73&ceid=9512399)

| | | Friend, We’ve talked a lot about how Alaskans value freedom and independence. So this Independence Day, we want to know what freedom means to you. [**Take our quick survey to let us know what freedoms you’re thankful for today →**](https://click.everyaction.com/k/65952322/414935547/1877289402?utm_campaign=em_230704_freedom&utm_source=em&utm_medium=em&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzEwMjc1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNTcxMjA1ZC1hYTFhLWVlMTEtYTliYi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sLKTbFx8oBYgCr05QiPh24axTdlFyjqKiz9ZcHeJ8FY=&emci=a4065aec-a61a-ee11-a9bb-00224832eb73&emdi=1571205d-aa1a-ee11-a9bb-00224832eb73&ceid=9512399) | [**LET US KNOW »** ](https://click.everyaction.com/k/65952323/414935548/1877289402?utm_campaign=em_230704_freedom&utm_source=em&utm_medium=em&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzEwMjc1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNTcxMjA1ZC1hYTFhLWVlMTEtYTliYi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sLKTbFx8oBYgCr05QiPh24axTdlFyjqKiz9ZcHeJ8FY=&emci=a4065aec-a61a-ee11-a9bb-00224832eb73&emdi=1571205d-aa1a-ee11-a9bb-00224832eb73&ceid=9512399)
---
Maybe you value the freedom to make your own health care decisions.
Or maybe you value your privacy or freedom from government intrusion.
Or perhaps you’re thankful for freedom from oppression and tyranny.
Today we commemorate the 247th year since the signing of the Declaration of Independence, but Alaska has only been a state for 64 years. Up here, you can feel it in your bones: **Freedom is not to be taken for granted.[So whatever type of freedom you’re celebrating today, take a second to let us know by filling out our 30-second survey!](https://click.everyaction.com/k/65952324/414935549/1877289402?utm_campaign=em_230704_freedom&utm_source=em&utm_medium=em&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzEwMjc1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNTcxMjA1ZC1hYTFhLWVlMTEtYTliYi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sLKTbFx8oBYgCr05QiPh24axTdlFyjqKiz9ZcHeJ8FY=&emci=a4065aec-a61a-ee11-a9bb-00224832eb73&emdi=1571205d-aa1a-ee11-a9bb-00224832eb73&ceid=9512399)**
[**TAKE THE SURVEY »** ](https://click.everyaction.com/k/65952325/414935550/1877289402?utm_campaign=em_230704_freedom&utm_source=em&utm_medium=em&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxNi8xLzEwMjc1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxNTcxMjA1ZC1hYTFhLWVlMTEtYTliYi0wMDIyNDgzMmViNzMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=sLKTbFx8oBYgCr05QiPh24axTdlFyjqKiz9ZcHeJ8FY=&emci=a4065aec-a61a-ee11-a9bb-00224832eb73&emdi=1571205d-aa1a-ee11-a9bb-00224832eb73&ceid=9512399)

Thank you!
Team BAFAF

[**July 4, 1776:** The American colonies officially separated from Great Britain when the Second Continental Congress unanimously adopted the Declaration of Independence. The anniversary of this historic event is marked by Independence Day, which became a federal holiday in 1870. ](https://click.historyoftheday.com/u/click?_t=930f96a60371422687dbefcfca24c32b&_m=3eefb71e6dc449978629f045db351062&_e=1wCtqNt5mGJgWa0VDF8pHDsafP-Jo0sbY7qO60tWkfijyKTVzeS7wsDWzGmhh3nCzsL0UywoRTDgORzZBk4e3RetB6hCoAOu2kwH-u1p3TrmlrwityvrqgiYiypItwFeQEhnIlKV9PsV-9LCEjPh0WGJvGjLJPxg23vFfxuxpYhNybLrSuoBvTc0tH0L-xM57RdPhdk7Jfzf4Qnb3w_eCqPMYymje44PqT-D1WGX-GD0gGw54lsYl916uPqte53w) **Source -[loc.gov](https://click.historyoftheday.com/u/click?_t=930f96a60371422687dbefcfca24c32b&_m=3eefb71e6dc449978629f045db351062&_e=1wCtqNt5mGJgWa0VDF8pHDsafP-Jo0sbY7qO60tWkfijyKTVzeS7wsDWzGmhh3nCzsL0UywoRTDgORzZBk4e3fXDARmk6eAp7uWK6saLEYd9KBGqYHEIkJNRtgxf0xvX2LkTktu4dWQpPn3m01rmq5Ptiij992RLf_myegFA5DjP5En_0DEdL0DwyVraoYLoYVg4bL2ziATxNTTEZWEKMXtQel-uDHe3B-emgMnJwRbOTea6gZs61nbewNDq4qn-)**

[**July 4, 1826:** On the 50th anniversary of American Independence, two former U.S. Presidents and signers of the Declaration of Independence, John Adams and Thomas Jefferson, passed away. Jefferson died first at the age of 83 at his home in Monticello, Virginia, and several hours later, Adams died at the age of 90 in Quincy, Massachusetts. According to reports, Adams’ last words were, “Jefferson still lives.” ](https://click.historyoftheday.com/u/click?_t=930f96a60371422687dbefcfca24c32b&_m=3eefb71e6dc449978629f045db351062&_e=1wCtqNt5mGJgWa0VDF8pHDsafP-Jo0sbY7qO60tWkfgJgKLRQt7Brp9B6PaxgRomSt4f-mcp1bso4BtUzt9agpRoSB46xcoBYQ4dWDP53W1ZMqZlcTyNDCBvZ-OWqbKVlJr1UAa-M5ln2gLcpWKUPOiqcP-3BHo0eH9MwZ89P7PQZLCiKoIIYyGMgWg1IUAquOuB0PnFUTGfQIino4wZ_51-dNCyki28wx09FllEY_IjPMw1qHfo_Y-sZ8-ZvGpw) **Source -[loc.gov](https://click.historyoftheday.com/u/click?_t=930f96a60371422687dbefcfca24c32b&_m=3eefb71e6dc449978629f045db351062&_e=1wCtqNt5mGJgWa0VDF8pHDsafP-Jo0sbY7qO60tWkfgJgKLRQt7Brp9B6PaxgRomSt4f-mcp1bso4BtUzt9agpRoSB46xcoBYQ4dWDP53W3pB5VJCPSFJWtQhGOqpvp8c2cgCjEvuOovJv20dJGT7zw0EJ78Ut8jHglPm2gUuBN2aM9mE_XkykgP7fMIyXX1HYcQhGNUx_WNfrG5CwrisfdEvgUAR57BgyfLJlQNlIh6LV3LViYrUzoR7cUhCHy6)**
